dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Hey everybody, it's Haitch, and welcome to the latest installment of Dune Pod, your one stop shop to get ready for the new Dune movie, or just talking about great movies if you don't care about Dune. This week, I'm joined as always by my co-host Jason.

dialogues.name: JASON

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH

dialogues.text: Wowww. Far out.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: On this episode we discuss Paul Thomas Anderson's bizarre paranoia noir film, Inherent Vice starring Dune's Josh Brolin. We cover the importance of good hygiene, how the current era has us on edge and debate appropriate drug use. If you're enjoying the show, check out our Discord server where you can hang out with us whenever you want. A link is in the show notes. And now without further a-Dune, Inherent Vice. [cheesy sitcom music plays] Dune Pod is recorded in front of a live studio audience.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Inherent Vice is a dark journey through the inevitable collapse of the 60s countercultural revolution under the crushing weight of the resurgence of a conservative backlash in America. Larry "Doc" Sportello, a private eye in Gordita Beach, a suburb of LA, has his life up ended when his ex girlfriend goes missing alongside her new boyfriend. The powerful real estate magnate turned New Age drug dabbler, Mickey Wolfman. Doc will embark on a journey to find his ex old lady that will lead him into conflict with white supremacists banker gangs, Russian loan sharks, drug addled dentists, and always the malevolent forces of the LAPD and their Renaissance cop, Lieutenant detective Christian F. Bigfoot Bjornsen. Will dock untangle the mysterious web, find his girl and continue a trip laced with spice? Or will he inevitably be caught up in the storm, his very life a case of Inherent Vice? [Journey Through the Past by Neil Young and the Stray Gators fades out]
